abstract: A flag that indicates the user should be prompted for a passphrase on import or export.
overview:
## Discussion

When set, the password for import or export is obtained by user prompt. (A password is sometimes referred to as a passphrase to emphasize the fact that a longer string that includes non-letter characters, such as numbers, punctuation, and spaces, is more secure than a simple word.) Otherwise, you must provide the password in the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecItemImportExportKeyParameters/passphrase] field of the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecItemImportExportKeyParameters] structure. A user-supplied password is preferred, because it avoids having the cleartext password appear in the application’s address space at any time.

# securePassphrase

**Type Property**

A flag that indicates the user should be prompted for a passphrase on import or export.

## Declaration

```swift
static var securePassphrase: SecKeyImportExportFlags { get }
```

## Discussion

When set, the password for import or export is obtained by user prompt. (A password is sometimes referred to as a passphrase to emphasize the fact that a longer string that includes non-letter characters, such as numbers, punctuation, and spaces, is more secure than a simple word.) Otherwise, you must provide the password in the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecItemImportExportKeyParameters/passphrase] field of the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecItemImportExportKeyParameters] structure. A user-supplied password is preferred, because it avoids having the cleartext password appear in the application’s address space at any time.
title: securePassphrase
